extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) is VIOLATED. The vulnerability is transition [t_resume_glitch] (line 25): on send_attempt, if (opted_out and stage >= 4), it sets sent := true. The lock combination to reach stage 4 from stage 0 is key_1 (adv_0: 0->1), key_0 (adv_1: 1->2), key_0 (adv_2: 2->3), key_1 (adv_3: 3->4). Crucially, t_optout (line 10) sets opted_out := true but does NOT reset stage, and none of the adv_* transitions guard on opted_out, so the lock can be advanced to stage 4 while already opted out. Trace of witness sequence [optout, key_1, key_0, key_0, key_1, send_attempt]: (1) optout -> opted_out=T, sent=F, stage=0; (2) key_1/adv_0 -> stage=1; (3) key_0/adv_1 -> stage=2; (4) key_0/adv_2 -> stage=3; (5) key_1/adv_3 -> stage=4; (6) send_attempt -> t_send_allowed disabled (opted_out true), t_send_blocked disabled (requires stage<4), t_resume_glitch ENABLED (opted_out and stage>=4) -> sent := true. Final reachable state opted_out=T, sent=T violates the property. Length 6 events, within the 7-event bound. The three send_attempt transitions are mutually exclusive by guard, so no priority ambiguity.
